response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile software developer with strong web and full-stack credentials, but he does not meet the core requirements of this role. There is no indication of Japanese language ability (a stated native-level requirement), no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience, no embedded networking or LAN-switch background, and no exposure to the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, captive portal). His C experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments and a thesis project; his career centers on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang for web platforms. The gap between his profile and this embedded security firmware role is too large to justify an interview.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling, and open-source contributions
Some systems-level exposure through DLang low-latency services and C/C++ work at National Instruments
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed, which is a hard requirement for the role
No experience with Linux kernel modification, embedded firmware, or LAN-switch/networking technologies (L2/L3, SNMP, NETCONF, MACsec, 802.1X)
C for UNIX is not a demonstrated core skill; recent work is almost entirely TypeScript/Node.js web development

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security technology experience
Long, solid general software engineering track record
